sourceMovie.title: Hairspray
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Hairspray (2007) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Sierra Burgess Is a Loser
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hairspray clicked for you, Sierra Burgess Is a Loser (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Step Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hairspray clicked for you, Step Up (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hairspray?
faq.answer: Try Sierra Burgess Is a Loser, Step Up, The Dreamers—each shares DNA with Hairspray while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hairspray?
faq.answer: Hairspray (2007) is best described as Comedy, Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hairspray worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
